commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a CLI-level hard timeout for explicit local `openclaw agent --local` runs and tests finite timeout and `--timeout 0` behavior in `agent-via-gateway`.

PR surface: Source +97, Tests +68. Total +165 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence reproduction is included. Source inspection shows the current local path awaits `agentCommand` directly, and the PR's mocked test reproduces a never-settling local promise, but exact-head real CLI proof is still missing.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer policy review and contributor live proof rather than an automated code repair; no concrete diff bug was found.

target.title: fix(cli): enforce local agent hard timeout
target.body:
## Problem

`openclaw agent --local --timeout N` can still leave the CLI alive until an outer wrapper timeout kills it. The lower-level embedded agent timeout is useful, but it is not a command-level wall-clock guarantee: setup, retries, cleanup, or a wedged embedded run can still keep the CLI process around after the operator-specified timeout window.

## Change and value

This PR adds a CLI-level hard timeout for explicit local embedded agent runs. When `--local` is set, the CLI now arms a wall-clock timer for `--timeout N` plus 30 seconds of grace. If that timer fires, the CLI aborts the local run signal and exits with code `124`, matching the exit code operators already see from GNU `timeout` wrappers.

The value is that scripts no longer need to wait for their own much larger timeout window just because the embedded run failed to settle cleanly.

## Who is affected, and who is not

- Affected: operators and automation running `openclaw agent --local` with a finite `--timeout` or configured agent timeout.
- Not affected: gateway-backed `openclaw agent` requests. They keep the existing gateway request timeout and embedded fallback behavior.
- Not affected: `--timeout 0`; that continues to mean no CLI hard timeout.
- Not fixed here: post-success event-loop handles that keep the process alive after a successful local result. That is covered separately by #86264.

## Why now

Local diagnostics showed the second CLI-exit class directly: an `agent --local` run can fail to produce a final result before the outer 120-second wrapper kills it, even when the inner `--timeout` is shorter. This patch makes the local CLI boundary own that wall-clock guarantee instead of relying only on the embedded runner to unwind itself.

## Implementation

- Creates a local-run abort signal composed from the existing SIGINT/SIGTERM bridge plus a CLI hard-timeout timer.
- Uses `--timeout N + 30s` as the hard deadline, with `--timeout 0` disabling the timer.
- On timeout, aborts the signal, writes a concise timeout error, and exits through `runtime.exit(124)`.
- Leaves gateway request timeout behavior unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw agent --local --timeout N` should have a command-level wall-clock timeout when the local run ignores or fails to settle after lower-level aborts.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux/WSL from `openclaw/openclaw` main at `b5c1199217e550e6f8bec674820618739505552d`, branch head `f32f963c4cb6b7df5df7a048a3bea44763029696`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=10000`

Evidence after fix: The affected command test file passed: `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts` 49/49. The new regression covers a local agent promise that never settles, verifies the composed abort signal is aborted at `--timeout 2 + 30s`, verifies `runtime.exit(124)`, and verifies `--timeout 0` does not arm the hard timer.

Observed result after fix: The modeled local CLI timeout path now exits at the command boundary instead of waiting indefinitely for the local agent promise to settle.

What was not tested: A real live `openclaw agent --local --timeout N` run that wedges and exits with 124 from this branch. This PR is draft until maintainers accept this policy shape or we add live exact-head proof.

## Verification

- `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts`: 49/49 passed under `test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Relationship to #86264

#86264 handles the post-completion cleanup class: a command has finished but deferred context-engine maintenance keeps work alive. This PR handles the no-completion class: a local agent command never settles, so the CLI needs a hard wall-clock boundary.

AI-assisted: yes.
